comment.text: This translation key describes the default signing algorithm used by an Identity Provider. It is displayed as a label in a combo box used to select or configure this setting in the Single Sign-On settings page.

usage.context:
<CheckboxSet prefix={prefix} />
 
 {provider === "IdentityProvider" ? (
 <SsoComboBox
 isDisabled={isDisabledIdpSigning}
 labelText={t("idpSigningAlgorithm")}
 name="idpVerifyAlgorithm"
 options={verifyAlgorithmsOptions}
 tabIndex={14}
 value={idpVerifyAlgorithm}
 dataTestId="idp_signing_combobox"

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The Arabic translation means 'Default algorithm for verifying login' instead of 'Default Sign Verifying Algorithm'. It incorrectly refers to 'login' (تسجيل الدخول) instead of 'signing' (التوقيع).
The word 'Default' is missing from the Spanish translation, which changes the meaning from a specific setting (the default one) to a general description of a signature verification algorithm.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: Algoritmo de verificación de firma predeterminado

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The translation uses 'signe' (which means 'sign' as in a symbol or gesture) instead of 'signature' (the cryptographic process). In the context of an Identity Provider and signing algorithms, 'signature' is the correct technical term.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: Algorithme de vérification de la signature par défaut

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The Italian translation uses 'segni' (signs/marks) instead of 'firma' (signature). In a technical context regarding Identity Providers and SSO, 'Sign Verifying' refers to digital signatures, not physical or general signs.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: Algoritmo di verifica della firma predefinito

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The Lao translation translates to 'Default signing verification process' instead of 'Default Sign Verifying Algorithm'. 'Algorithm' (ອັນກໍຣິທຶມ) is missing and replaced by 'Process' (ຂັ້ນຕອນ).
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: ອັນກໍຣິທຶມການຢືນຢັນການເຊັນເລີ່ມຕົ້ນ

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The Latvian translation 'Noklusējuma zīmju verifikācijas algoritms' translates to 'Default signature verification algorithm'. While close, the English 'Default Sign Verifying Algorithm' refers to the algorithm used for the *signing* process (the action of creating the signature), whereas the Latvian translation focuses on the *verification* of existing signatures. In the context of an Identity Provider setting, this is a critical distinction between the algorithm used to sign tokens and the algorithm used to verify them.
The translation introduces completely unrelated content ('Verificação dos logins' / 'Login verification') that is not present in the English source, which refers to a 'Sign Verifying Algorithm'.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: Algoritmo de Verificação de Assinatura Padrão

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The Russian translation uses 'Стандартный' (Standard) instead of 'Default' (По умолчанию) in a technical configuration context, and adds a colon ':' which is not present in the source.

The translation 'පෙරනිමි සත්‍යාපන ඇල්ගොරිතම' means 'Default Verification Algorithm'. It misses the word 'Sign' (සංඥා කිරීම/අත්සන් කිරීම), which is crucial for distinguishing between a general verification algorithm and a digital signing algorithm in an Identity Provider context.

The Slovenian translation uses 'Privzeti algoritem' (Default algorithm - singular) instead of 'Privzeti algoritem za preverjanje podpisov' (Default signing algorithm). More importantly, 'znakov' means 'signs/characters' instead of 'podpisov' (signatures), which changes the technical meaning from 'signing' to 'verifying characters'.
